feature_definitions.sales_lag_1: units_sold from 1 prior row in the item/store series
feature_definitions.sales_lag_7: units_sold from 7 prior rows in the item/store series
feature_definitions.sales_lag_28: units_sold from 28 prior rows in the item/store series
feature_definitions.sales_rolling_mean_7: mean units_sold over the prior 7 rows after shift(1)
feature_definitions.sales_rolling_mean_28: mean units_sold over the prior 28 rows after shift(1)
feature_definitions.sales_rolling_std_28: sample standard deviation of units_sold over the prior 28 rows after shift(1)
feature_definitions.price_lag_7: sell_price from 7 prior rows in the item/store series
feature_definitions.historical_price_mean_28: mean available sell_price over the prior 28 rows after shift(1); missing prices remain missing and are not backfilled
feature_definitions.day_of_week: date weekday number where Monday=0 and Sunday=6
feature_definitions.week_of_year: ISO calendar week number from date
feature_definitions.is_weekend: 1 when day_of_week is Saturday or Sunday, otherwise 0
feature_definitions.snap_active: current California SNAP indicator copied from snap_CA

leakage_controls: Rows are grouped by item_id and store_id and ordered by date.
All demand lags use shift and all rolling demand windows start with shift(1).
Rolling windows are trailing only and never centered.
Price lag and rolling price history use past rows only; prices are never backfilled.
Features are calculated over each full chronological series before eligibility filtering.
Existing train, validation, and test labels are preserved; no random split is used.
Current sell_price is allowed as known candidate-price context; units_sold is target only.
model_training_status: No model was trained on Day 12.
